items.title: A New Frame on Emotion Regulation in Aging: The Adaptive Positive Tactic Shift
items.author: Derek M. Isaacowitz, Blake D. Ebright-Jones, Rebecca J. PolkDepartment of Psychological & Brain Sciences, Washington University in St. Louis
items.content: <article>Current Directions in Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. Older adults report higher levels of emotional well-being in cross-sectional studies. Despite assertions that older adults are better at regulating emotions, studies investigating emotion regulation (ER)strategieshave not found consistent age ...</article>

items.title: Dimensions of Stereotypes About Groups
items.author: Vincent Yzerbyt, Alex Koch, Marco Brambilla, Naomi Ellemers, Susan T. Fiske, Gandalf Nicolas
items.content: <article>Current Directions in Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. People perceive social groups along stereotype dimensions. Several models of social evaluation identify so-called horizontal (relational, warmth, communion) and vertical (achievement, competence, agency) judgments, also known as the Big Two. Each has two ...</article>

items.title: Abstract Cognitive Maps for Complex Social Systems
items.author: Oriel FeldmanHall, Jae-Young Son, Apoorva Bhandari
items.content: <article>Current Directions in Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. How do people represent social networks in their minds? Inspired by work on spatial navigation, recent research reveals how people use domain-general computational principles to build cognitive maps for navigating their social environments. However, some ...</article>

items.title: A Temporal Hierarchy of Sustained Attention Dynamics
items.author: Monica D. Rosenberg
items.content: <article>Current Directions in Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. Maintaining focus is a critical component of attention. Our ability to do so, however, changes over time—developing across the life span, declining as tasks drag on, fluctuating from one second to the next, and even oscillating at the subsecond level. ...</article>
